substep.title: Draft Current State Section
substep.action:
From research file findings, draft the Current state section: - Map the touched-area module/types/seams - Cite specific file:line references - Name patterns and invariants that exist - Stay focused: only the touched area, not the whole codebase
Apply the tag-specific guidance already in the skeleton (the skeleton's "_Guidance ({{primary_tag}})_" line is the scaffold; you fill the content).

substep.title: Write to Design File
substep.action: Edit {{design_path}} — replace the "_(populated by code-design through brain-surgery conversation)_" placeholder under "## Current state" with the drafted content. Preserve the guidance line as a comment or remove it (your call — the section's purpose is now captured in the content itself).

substep.title: Present and Stress-Test

check.action: Lock the section. Proceed to step 3.
Revise the section. Re-draft. Re-present. Loop within this step until alignment.
This is a foundational reversal. Increment {{strike_count}}. See substep 2d.
Acknowledge the reframe. Note the invalidated work (research turns that no longer apply, design draft now stale). Re-draft current state per the new framing. Proceed.

check.ask:
Second foundational reversal in this D-phase. The accumulated design draft is becoming inconsistent.
Continue absorbing, or pause to consolidate? - Continue (Recommended for small reframes): I'll revise and proceed - Pause and consolidate: stop here; review what we have on disk; restart D-phase with the new framing

Third foundational reversal. The original D-phase scoping is increasingly clearly wrong.
Recommendation: abort current D-phase. Archive the current bundle as research evidence. Restart D-phase with the new understanding.
- Abort + restart (Recommended) - Continue anyway: I'll revise and proceed (the bundle's coherence is your responsibility from here)

check.action: Halt the skill. The bundle on disk is preserved as evidence. The codesmith primary agent surfaces the abort to the user with a restart recommendation.
